title: hedera helix subsp. caucasigena
intro: Hedera helix subsp. caucasigena, also known as Caucasian ivy, is a subspecies of the common ivy plant, characterized by its smaller leaf size and more compact growth habit compared to the typical English ivy. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing stress, improving sleep, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Caucasian ivy is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although its efficacy and safety are not extensively studied, and further research is recommended. As a result, caution is advised when using this plant, and it should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional or herbalist.
